source: TBPN — SpaceX IPO, token path, sovereign AI won't reach frontier
summary: China's AI advantage via distillation of Western frontier models is eroding as labs lock down reasoning traces and stop releasing frontier models openly
summary_extended: Baker argues that China's AI competitive advantage — built on industrial-scale distillation of Western frontier models — is structurally eroding as leading Western labs move to lock down their reasoning traces and cease open releases of frontier models. He cites DeepSeek as the canonical example, noting it was built from approximately 160,000 reasoning traces derived from OpenAI's o1/o3 models, and that Chinese actors run roughly 100,000 API endpoints continuously distilling from available Western models. The core thesis is that this distillation arbitrage only works while frontier models remain accessible, and that a shift toward closed, proprietary releases (he references a model called 'Mythos' as an early signal) will cut off this vector. The investment implication is that Western frontier AI labs — which are predominantly private — retain durable moats if they successfully close off distillation pathways, while the structural catch-up dynamic that made Chinese AI appear competitive may prove more fragile than consensus assumes. Baker presents no directly actionable public equity trade, as the primary actors (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek) are all private.
detail: Baker notes China became very good at industrial-scale distillation (e.g., DeepSeek built from ~160K reasoning traces from o1/o3). They run 100K endpoints distilling across every available API. But this advantage disappears if frontier models stop being released openly. Mythos (likely Mythic or a frontier model) is a sign of things to come in locking down distillation.
confidence_evidence: Baker presents this as a structural trend with specific data points (160K reasoning traces) but some details are garbled in transcript
quote_fragment: all that goes away if people stop releasing these models at the frontier
